response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine language-tooling interest (D test runners, assertion libraries) and direct IDE development experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI). However, the role requires strong Java and Kotlin expertise with deep JVM understanding and solid Spring ecosystem experience — the candidate's career is centered on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with only incidental Java exposure and no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or JVM-internals work. The gap on core required qualifications is too large for a senior IC position focused on enterprise JVM tooling.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and language ecosystems (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
Broad, long-tenured engineering background with strong testing and craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No meaningful Java/Kotlin or JVM-internals experience; core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No exposure to Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM frameworks and application servers
No evidence of working on large-scale JVM codebases or build-tool integration (Gradle/Maven)

key_factors.factor: Prior IDE internals and tooling work
